cards.title: Scorching Sands
cards.narrative: Year 440. A year into the war in Konfurtah against the Kingdom of the Undying Scorpion, the conflict is proving excruciatingly difficult. Widram is a sharp tactician who knows the land well: we lose more ground than we gain. But the most terrifying threat is what we cannot see. Our fallen soldiers are vanishing from the battlegrounds, their bodies nowhere to be found. Rumors suggest the enemy is desecrating the corpses, draining them of blood to survive the harsh desert, possibly taking advantage of the frequent sandstorms to act undisturbed. Our troops are petrified. According to the Cult's teachings, without proper burial a soul cannot return to the Mother's womb, nor reincarnate into this world. The thought of eternal severance from the cycle of life weighs heavily on our soldiers' morale, threatening to break their resolve.

cards.title: Terror in the Ranks
cards.narrative: We push forward and cross another of the enemy's defensive lines, but leave the bodies of our fallen compatriots behind. As the corpses of our dead soldiers keep disappearing, rumors spread through the ranks that Widram feeds his soldiers with the blood of our comrades and turns victories into cruel banquets. We cannot confirm whether this is scaremonger propaganda or not, but morale is at an all-time low. Our soldiers do not fear death, but dread not being properly buried and thus severed from the rebirth cycle of the Mother. Desertions increase, and as Queen Serena presses our troops to proceed further, our ranks get thinner and we start to lose more and more battles.

cards.title: Silver Mallet
cards.narrative: The bodies of our fallen soldiers keep vanishing in the sands, but—to the great relief of our troops—the Council recreates the Silver Mallet, a legendary institution devoted to fighting unnatural threats in the name of Ankist and the Mother. Battling Sisters are trained in the ways of the war hammer, whilst the most experienced knights are indoctrinated in the divine truths of the Cult to shield them from the perils of fear. Most are immediately deployed to assist in the war effort, while a few remain home to inspire and reassure our people. Galvanized by the Mallet's presence, our troops' spirit is lifted. May the tide of the war turn in our favor as we hastily march towards Nidar Ilak, bastion of the Undying Scorpion!

cards.title: The Fin
cards.narrative: By leaving the Sweet Beyond market unregulated in Solad, control of its production and distribution has fallen into the hands of a newly emerged criminal syndicate known as the Fin. Sweet Beyond is spreading rapidly among society's poorest, who gather in droves at the port of Roinn, engaging in lewd and disruptive behavior that unsettles the populace. To fund their addictions, many impoverished users are also turning to crime, making the whole of Roinn a dangerous area and damaging the local economy. This poses a serious threat to public safety, but efforts to locate the Fin's production facilities and disrupt the black market have proven fruitless.

cards.title: Sirens
cards.narrative: Now that we control its largest laboratory, we finally uncover the secret surrounding Sweet Beyond: the substance is derived from the humors of previously unknown marine animals. Our scholars think they use these humors as a defense mechanism to distract potential predators by awakening their sexual instincts, diverting them to look for a mating partner instead of engaging in hunting. Given the vaguely feminine forms of these beasts, they may be the origin of the myth of the Sirens. The extraction process involves harvesting a gland found within the brains of these "Sirens," only accessible after killing them. As our scholars delve deeper into the nature of this substance, they are beginning to ponder the other potential applications it might possess.

cards.title: Fear in Coden
cards.narrative: As we chose to ignore them, the murders in Coden continue—six more women die. There are too many coincidences to dismiss the same, morbid mind behind all of them: all victims are golden-eyed women, each one horribly cut open in the same spots—legs, abdomen, neck, back—as if the killer were searching for something. The selection of victims has also changed, now including farmers, artisans, and young noblewomen. The people of Coden try to go about their business, but it's hard to ignore the roaming murderer, now known as the "Scourge of Coden." Even the Royal Jester Bambleass repeats the macabre rhyme, "Golden Eyes, Short Lives."
